title: Review P1 fix: scope cycle refusal to cycles the write introduces or participates in
action: assertNoCycle currently throws when detectCycles finds ANY cycle in the union of the whole on-disk store + incoming cells, so a pre-existing legacy cycle in feature A blocks unrelated adds in feature B — contradicting locked D2 (pre-existing cycles are REPORTED by cells schedule, the write guard only stops INTRODUCING/participating in one). Fix: after detectCycles, refuse only cycles whose member set intersects the incoming cell ids; pre-existing unrelated cycles no longer block, still reported by schedule diagnostics. Add regression tests: (1) pre-existing on-disk cycle in another feature does NOT block adding an acyclic cell; (2) introducing a new cycle is still refused; (3) an update that joins an existing cycle is still refused. Mirror byte-identically to .bee/bin/lib/cells.mjs.

trace.verification_evidence: RED (before): assertNoCycle refused on ANY store cycle — reproduced by architecture reviewer in a temp store (featureA cycle blocked unrelated featureB add). GREEN (after): new test proves unrelated writes succeed over a legacy on-disk cycle while participating/introducing writes stay refused; 334/0.
trace.verify_output: test_lib: 334 passed, 0 failed (incl new legacy-cycle regression: unrelated add + unrelated deps patch succeed over a hand-written on-disk cycle; a patch keeping a cycle member inside the cycle still refused; a cycle-breaking patch applies). test_lib_mirror: templates/lib and .bee/bin/lib byte-identical (17 files); hook inventory byte-identical (9 files).
